summary: QA review accepted FOUNDATION-002. Root strict TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier, lockfile, and architecture boundary gates are real and pass; the architecture checker self-test catches the violating sim-core fixture; reviewed scope stays within allowed implementation files plus lead-owned orchestration metadata.

tests_run.result: Confirmed branch chore/foundation-002-quality-boundaries; implementation changes are package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, tsconfig*.json, eslint.config.mjs, prettier.config.mjs, and tools/architecture/**, with lead-owned project metadata and route/handoff files present.
No PROJECT_STATUS.md or accepted ADR changes.
Prettier checked root package/config files and tools/architecture files; all matched files use Prettier code style.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 1 expected violation.
Initial parallel review batch hit pnpm EPERM during concurrent dependency materialization on Windows before ESLint executed; rerun serially below passed.
ESLint root gate passed for apps, packages, tools, eslint.config.mjs, and prettier.config.mjs.
Composed root gate ran format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check successfully.
Untracked files are the expected new config, tools/architecture, route, and handoff files; pnpm-generated node_modules from review validation was removed.

decisions: ACCEPT: root package scripts are real quality gates and no longer delegate to package-level hello placeholders.
ACCEPT: tsconfig.base.json enables the frozen strict baseline options, root tsconfig extends it, and current workspace sources under apps, packages, and tools are covered.

ACCEPT: architecture checker validates TypeScript baseline, scans sim-core/core imports and package manifests for forbidden platform/presentation dependencies, and self-tests a violating React import fixture.
risks: Existing package-level hello scripts remain outside this task scope; acceptance is based on root gates bypassing those placeholders.
Boundary checker covers current M0 rules and should be expanded as additional architecture packages are scaffolded.
requested_action: Record QA acceptance for FOUNDATION-002 and route the next lead-owned workflow step.
